differences.title: 1. DATA LOADING
differences.leftDesc: Server functions fetch data before rendering. Optimized for server execution.
differences.rightDesc: Supports server and client-side data fetching. Flexible for different needs.

differences.title: 2. RENDERING STRATEGY
differences.leftDesc: Primarily renders on the server. Less client-side JavaScript.
differences.rightDesc: Offers SSR, SSG, and ISR. Static generation for speed.

differences.title: 3. ROUTING
differences.leftDesc: Handles layouts and data with nested routes. Elegant structure.
differences.rightDesc: Automatic routing based on file structure. Simple and intuitive.

differences.title: 4. FORM HANDLING
differences.leftDesc: Leverages native HTML forms for submissions. Enhanced UX.
differences.rightDesc: Requires dedicated form components for mutations. More control.

differences.title: 5. CSS APPROACH
differences.leftDesc: Uses CSS Modules by default. Scoped styles.
differences.rightDesc: Supports various styling options, including CSS-in-JS and modules.

differences.title: 6. DEPLOYMENT
differences.leftDesc: Requires a Node.js server environment for runtime.
differences.rightDesc: Optimized for Vercel Edge, serverless functions. Global deployment.

differences.title: 7. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: State often managed on the server. Less client-side complexity.
differences.rightDesc: Relies more on client-side state management solutions.
